mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or pinned near zero, the discount rate on equity cash flows stays suppressed and liquidity conditions support higher S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is falsified if easing episodes do not coincide with positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If triggered by a falling FEDFUNDS path, SPX 20-day returns are negative or below the unconditional median in more than 60% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in CPIAUCSL above the recent trend forces real-rate repricing and raises the probability of tighter policy, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should appear when inflation momentum is clearly positive, not when inflation is flat.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.4%
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL acceleration episodes are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns more than 55% of the time, or the average excess return is not negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE and slowing PAYEMS together signal labor-market deterioration, weaker future demand, and lower earnings expectations, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is not valid if unemployment rises but payroll growth remains strong.
failure_condition: If joint labor-weakness states do not produce lower-than-baseline SPX 20-day returns, or if either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone fully explains the effect, reject the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in RSAFS relative to its recent level signals weaker consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should depress S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon. The signal should be strongest when sales growth turns negative from positive.
failure_condition: If two-month RSAFS declines are not followed by negative or below-median SPX 20-day returns in most cases, or the hit rate is below 45%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO reflects soft industrial output and weaker business-cycle momentum, which should weigh on equity prices over the next quarter. The relationship should be clearer when industrial production has already rolled over from a prior peak.
failure_condition: If negative INDPRO trend periods do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns or the rolling 3-year rank correlation stays near zero, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in HOUST from depressed levels signals improving housing demand, construction activity, and downstream consumption, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when housing starts are recovering after a prior trough.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by more than 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: If housing-start recovery episodes are not followed by positive SPX 20-day returns more often than not, or the average return is not above baseline, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is rising and PAYEMS is simultaneously decelerating, the labor market is losing momentum and household income growth weakens, which should hurt S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through cyclical de-risking. The interaction matters because a single weak series without the other is less informative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months while PAYEMS growth falls below its 6-month average
failure_condition: If the joint trigger does not predict negative SPX 5-day returns or if the effect disappears when conditioning on only one labor series, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-inflation-scare tightening phase, CPIAUCSL running above trend together with FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold implies higher real discount rates and multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The claim fails if inflation is high but policy remains accommodative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1.0% and CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month average
failure_condition: If high-inflation/high-rate states do not precede negative SPX 60-day returns or the interaction term is not materially stronger than either proxy alone, reject.
